data.title: Walgreens Boots gains on report there are still `signs of life' in takeover talks
data.description:
Walgreens Boots (NASDAQ:WBA) rose 10% on a report that there are still signs of life with private equity firm Sycamore's takeover talks with the pharmacy chain. 

"I am hearing there are still potentially signs of life," CNBC's David Faber said on the business network.

Faber said late last month that any deal was "mostly dead."

Developing story ...

data.title: Allegion targets 2025 EPS of $7.65-$7.85 with stable institutional growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Allegion plc (NYSE:ALLE) Q4 2024

Management View

  • John Stone, CEO, highlighted the company's strong performance in the Americas segment, which delivered mid-single-digit growth for three consecutive quarters. He emphasized the benefits of a resilient institutional base and noted capital deployment efforts, including $137 million in tuck-in acquisitions during 2024 and $220 million in share repurchases.
  • Key product launches in 2024 included the Schlage XE360 series, a next-generation electronic lock, and the Von Duprin 70 series for high-performance exit devices. Allegion also became the first security solutions provider to support mobile credentials on Wear OS by Google smartwatches.
  • CEO Stone stated, "We're encouraged by trends in our Americas non-residential business exiting 2024" and forecasted continued growth backed by stable municipal bond issuance and institutional market strength.
  • CFO Mike Wagnes reported Q4 revenue of $945.6 million, with organic growth of 3.5%, primarily driven by price and volume. Adjusted EPS came in at $1.86, supported by operational performance and favorable tax impacts. Full-year 2024 operating margins expanded by 70 basis points, while available cash flow reached $582.9 million.

Outlook

  • The company projects 2025 total revenue growth of 1% to 3%, with organic growth of 1.5% to 3.5%. Growth in the Americas is expected to be in the low- to mid-single digits, supported by both residential and non-residential segments.
  • Adjusted EPS guidance for 2025 is forecasted at $7.65 to $7.85, representing growth of 1.5% to 4% year-over-year. The company anticipates a 17.5% tax rate at the midpoint.
  • Allegion's guidance includes current tariffs but does not account for potential new tariffs from Mexico. Pricing actions are planned to mitigate any additional cost impacts.

Financial Results

  • Q4 revenue was $945.6 million compared to analysts' estimate of $938.9 million, while adjusted EPS of $1.86 was compared to the estimate of $1.75.
  • The Americas segment achieved revenue of $750 million, with a 4.6% organic growth rate led by favorable pricing and volume. Residential business posted high-single-digit growth, while non-residential grew mid-single digits.
  • International segment revenue reached $195.6 million, but organic revenue declined 0.7% due to macroeconomic challenges in Germany and China. Adjusted operating margin decreased by 100 basis points in this segment.
  • The company's 2024 cash flow increased by 12.9% year-over-year, driven by higher earnings and working capital efficiency.

Q&A

  • Jeffrey Sprague, Vertical Research, asked about the price-cost productivity dynamic in Q4. CFO Wagnes explained that pricing remained stable, with timing adjustments in rebate accruals affecting the quarter. He added that the company anticipates pricing and productivity exceeding inflation in 2025.
  • Julian Mitchell, Barclays, inquired about operating margins in 2025. CFO Wagnes indicated expectations for margin expansion at the lower end of 50 to 100 basis points, with strong contributions from the Americas segment.
  • Timothy Wojs, Baird, sought clarity on non-residential growth drivers. CEO Stone highlighted robust institutional demand and favorable project activity, supported by municipal bond issuance and Dodge Indicators.
  • Brett Linzey, Mizuho, asked about potential impacts from Mexico tariffs. CEO Stone noted that pricing actions would be taken to offset costs, though details remain hypothetical at this stage.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on pricing impacts, institutional market stability, and potential tariff implications. Concerns centered on international macroeconomic challenges and residential demand variability.
  • Management's tone was confident, emphasizing capital deployment, product innovation, and strong execution in the Americas segment. CEO Stone's remarks showed optimism about 2025 growth opportunities, particularly in institutional markets.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts maintained similar skepticism regarding international headwinds, while management displayed consistent confidence in the company's resilience and growth strategy.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Adjusted EPS guidance increased from Q3's $7.35-$7.45 range to $7.65-$7.85 for 2025. Revenue growth projections remained stable, with slight adjustments to segment expectations.
  • Q4 results showed a slight decline in international performance compared to Q3's flat organic growth. However, the Americas segment maintained robust growth in both quarters.
  • Management's tone remained optimistic, with a focus on institutional growth and stable markets. Analyst inquiries showed continued interest in pricing dynamics and macroeconomic impacts across segments.

Risks and Concerns

  • Macroeconomic challenges in Germany and weaker demand in China pose ongoing risks to the international segment.
  • Potential tariffs on imports from Mexico could impact residential product costs, though management plans to implement pricing actions to mitigate effects.
  • Residential demand variability remains a concern, with underlying markets showing mixed signals for sustained growth.

Final Takeaway

Allegion delivered strong Q4 results, supported by robust growth in the Americas and solid operational execution. The company remains optimistic about 2025, with stable institutional markets, new product launches, and active M&A efforts driving growth. While macroeconomic challenges persist in international markets, Allegion's pricing strategy and disciplined capital deployment reinforce its long-term growth outlook.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: California Resources announces partial redemption of 7.125% senior notes due 2026
data.description:
• California Resources (NYSE:CRC) has issued a notice of partial redemption with respect to its 7.125% Senior Notes due 2026, it said on Tuesday.
  • On February 28, 2025, the company will redeem an aggregate principal amount of $123 million of notes in accordance with the terms set forth in the indenture governing the notes.
  • The notes will be redeemed at a redemption price of 100.000% of the principal amount thereof, plus accrued and unpaid interest, if any, to, but excluding, the redemption date.
  • Following the redemption, there will be an aggregate principal amount of $122.5 million of notes outstanding.

data.title: Dominari Holdings announces entry into U.S. AI data center market
data.description:
• Dominari Holdings (NASDAQ:DOMH) has announced the creation of American Data Centers, whose members include Dominari, Donald J. Trump Jr., Eric Trump and other industry professionals in the AI space.
  • This new venture is designed to address the growing demand for high-performance computing infrastructure. Dominari currently holds a 32% interest in ADC.
  • On February 11, the Company announced that its board of directors had declared a special cash dividend of $4M, in the aggregate, to its stockholders of record as of the close of business on February 24, 2025.
  • Following the recent closing of Dominari's financings, it has been determined that the special cash dividend will be $0.32/share, ~$4.6M in the aggregate, and is expected to be paid on March 3, 2025.
  • DOMH -17.6% to $9.83.

data.title: Cemtrex's Vicon subsidiary secures $1.2M order for border protection deployment in Texas
data.description:
• Vicon Industries (OTC:VCON), a subsidiary of Cemtrex (NASDAQ:CETX) said on Tuesday that it has secured a significant new order valued at approximately $1.2M from an existing customer.
  • The order includes Vicon cameras, servers, and additional Valerus VMS licenses for deployment in a Texas border protection application.
  • With fulfillment expected in fiscal Q2 2025, the order adds to the company’s accelerating momentum, positioning Vicon to meet or exceed its growth targets for the year.
  • Cemtrex (CETX) shares were up more than 2.3% in Tuesday trade.

data.title: BASF downgraded at Berenberg on valuation, natural gas prices
data.description:
BASF (OTCQX:BASFY) (OTCQX:BFFAF) on Monday was downgraded to Hold from a previous investment rating of Buy among analysts at financial-services firm Berenberg. They said the chemicals maker is more fairly valued after rallying about 22% since early January.

“BASF trades above mid-cycle enterprise value to earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization,” Sebastian Bray, analyst at Berenberg, said in a February 17 report. “We do not see upside to consensus EBITDA for 2025/26, especially given current highs in spot European natural gas prices. This is not to mention the lurking threat of tariffs.”

data.title: Windtree Therapeutics approves 1-for-50 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Windtree Therapeutics (NASDAQ:WINT) has approved a 1-for-50 reverse stock split of the company’s common stock.
  • The reverse stock split will become effective on February 20, 2025, and WINT’s common stock is expected to begin trading on a reverse stock split-adjusted basis on Nasdaq on February 21, 2025.
  • The reverse stock split is intended to increase the price per share of the company’s common stock to allow the company to demonstrate compliance with the $1 minimum bid price requirement for continued listing on Nasdaq.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Toppoint expanding into refrigeration logistics
data.description:
• Toppoint Holdings (NYSE:TOPP) has announced its expansion into refrigeration logistics, marking a key milestone in its diversification strategy.
  • Through a new strategic collaboration with a leading refrigerated logistics provider, specializing in the safe and efficient transport of temperature-sensitive goods.
  • Toppoint will manage an increasing supply of refrigerated containers at the ports of Newark, NJ, Philadelphia, PA, and Baltimore, MD.
  • This expansion enhances Toppoint’s operational resilience by securing a consistent pipeline of work, mitigating the volatility caused by fluctuations in ship volume and port traffic.
  • By entering the refrigeration logistics sector, Toppoint aims to establish more stable revenue streams while enhancing its service offerings for both new and existing clients.

data.title: HF Foods Group secures revolving credit agreement of up to $125 million
data.description:
• HF Foods Group (NASDAQ:HFFG) on Tuesday said it has amended its credit agreement with JPMorgan Chase Bank and Comerica Bank to add Wells Fargo as an additional lender, and increased the company's aggregate revolving commitment by $25 million to $125 million.
  • “This additional revolver capacity will help support our growth strategy and the continued execution of our strategic transformation plan by providing us with further financial flexibility,” said Felix Lin, President and CEO of HF Foods.
  • HFFG -4.83% premarket to $2.36.
  • Source: Press Release
